nativeRange.habitat: Outer reef rubble zones with flat or gently sloping bottoms and scattered low outcrops of rock and coral, typically at depths of 24–60 meters.

diet.notes: Generally does well on vitamin-enriched frozen mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, and other meaty foods supplemented with high-quality marine flake and pellet. Multiple small feedings per day are advised given the species' active metabolism.

summary: The Splendid Pintail Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus isosceles) is a western Pacific reef fish whose coloration — pink, yellow, and orange tones — intensifies or fades with mood. Generally considered peaceful and reef-safe, it is an active swimmer that requires a securely covered tank.
careNotes: The Splendid Pintail Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus isosceles) is found in the western Pacific from Japan (Izu-Oshima and the Ryukyu Archipelago) south through Taiwan (Green Island) to the Philippines, typically inhabiting outer-reef rubble zones at depths of 24–60 m. Terminal-phase males display the most intense coloration, with lavender, orange, and yellow tones that intensify further during courtship displays. This species has been documented jumping from open aquaria, and a tight-fitting lid is considered essential. A tank of at least 400 liters is generally advisable given the fish's active swimming nature and the territory a male requires. Males can be kept with females that are introduced simultaneously or prior to the male. Color intensity is mood-dependent and may fade or brighten naturally. The species is generally considered reef-safe and has not been reported to nip corals or invertebrates.
breedingNotes: Cirrhilabrus isosceles is a protogynous hermaphrodite, with females transitioning to terminal-phase males. Spawning in captivity has not been widely reported and is considered difficult to achieve in the home aquarium.

additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (10.0 cm SL, consistent with LiveAquaria's 5" TL), temperature range (22–27°C; LiveAquaria converts to 22.2–25.6°C — averaged to 22–26.5°C), native range (Japan, Ryukyu Archipelago, Taiwan, Philippines), habitat (outer reef rubble, 24–60 m depth), family Labridae, order Eupercaria. No SG or pH data available from FishBase. Reef2Reef wiki returned 403; BlueZoo Aquatics had no species-specific page.

fish.breedingNotes: Protogynous hermaphrodite. Captive spawning has not been widely documented. Keeping a single male with one or more females introduced simultaneously is the standard approach.
